data.title: Nintendo Switch 2 preorders delayed in America over Trump tariff concerns
data.description:
Nintendo (OTCPK:NTDOY) (OTCPK:NTDOF) is delaying preorders for its latest Switch 2 console over concerns of the impact of President Donald Trump’s reciprocal tariffs, The Verge reported Friday.

According to a statement sent to the technology news outlet by Eddie Garcia, who handles media relations for Nintendo of America, preorders will no longer begin on April 9 in the U.S.

"Pre-orders for Nintendo Switch 2 in the U.S. will not start April 9, 2025, in order to assess the potential impact of tariffs and evolving market conditions. Nintendo will update timing at a later date," Garcia reportedly said in the statement.

However, the launch date of June 5, 2025, is unchanged, Garcia told The Verge.

data.title: In the uniform business, Cintas is 'the only real choice' -- Barclays
data.description:
A focus on fundamentals is likely not a good thing for UniFirst (NYSE:UNF), as there is no longer the benefit of an acquisition premium, and “business as usual” is not only uninspiring, but at a risk of deteriorating,” leading Barclays analyst Manav Patnaik to downgrade the stock to Underweight from Equalweight.

In concert with a downgrade, Patnaik also lowered his target price for the uniform provider by 16% to $152, representing an 11% downside to Thursday’s closing price.

The company’s most recent quarterly results confirmed Patnaik’s concern that its current trajectory as a stand-alone business will likely produce future results that underscore poor organic growth and slow progression on strategic objectives.

Despite assurances by the company’s CEO on FQ2 earnings call Wednesday that investments in the business are “starting to show returns in several areas…capturing opportunities and creating value,” there were questions as to how they would achieve an “equivalent level of strategic value creation on a stand-alone basis.”

“We think UNF’s indicated inflection point is more likely not to be achieved,” Patnaik cautions.

Same goes for Vestis Corporation (VSTS), which is also assigned an Underweight rating in consideration of the challenges the company faces and loss of any acquisition premium in its multiple. Adding to Vestis’ “prohibitive strategic position” is flawed customer service, union exposure, high debt, and underinvestment in the business.

So, where should investors go?

“Cintas is the only real choice in uniforms,” Patnaik says. With an Overweight rating, Cintas (CTAS) remains the proven “growth staple” stock to own, not only in uniforms, but in broader industrials as well.”

While not immune to tariff shocks, Cintas’ (CTAS) ability to navigate their supply chain – given their track record during COVID – will remain at a strong competitive advantage to its peers, and if necessary, can be aided by certain price increases.

“Tariff impacts should be manageable given CTAS’s scaled and proven supply chain and will likely be more of a potential opportunity to put pressure on their competitors.”

Cintas (CTAS) has also enjoyed organic growth for 53 of 55 years, along with a strong balance sheet, which the company will use wisely in the form of buybacks and accretive small to medium acquisitions.

In Friday’s defensive market, shares of Cintas (CTAS) are outperforming with a loss of 3%, versus -7% for Vestis Corp (VSTS), and -5% for UniFirst (NYSE:UNF).

data.title: RFK Jr. to reinstate 20% of HHS staff laid off after DOGE errors
data.description:
After shedding as many as 10,000 full-time staffers at his department as part of a massive restructuring effort last week, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr., said he would reinstate 20% of them as mistakes from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) led to those layoffs.

“Personnel that should not have been cut were cut. We’re reinstating them,” RFK Jr. said Thursday, noting that some of the programs are also being resumed after an initial decision to terminate as part of the reorganization.

“That was always the plan,” RFK Jr. said, adding, “Part of the DOGE—we talked about this from the beginning—is we’re going to do 80% cuts, but 20% of those are going to have to be reinstalled because we’ll make mistakes.”

According to him, HHS programs to be reinstated include a project run by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to monitor blood lead levels in children.

HHS planned to cut 2,400 CDC employees, representing about 18% of the agency's total workforce, as part of the restructuring program, which got underway early this week after an initial announcement on Mar. 27.

data.title: Wall Street's fear gauge spikes to highest level since 2020 COVID crash
data.description:
Wall Street's volatility index on Friday hit levels not seen since the stock market crash during COVID-19. The spike in fear came amid an overall rout in equities sparked by the imposition of the steepest American tariffs in over a century.

The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) jumped as much as +51.43% to hit a session peak at 45.46 points. That's the highest reading since the VIX closed at 46.70 points on April 7, 2020.

On Thursday, the VIX (VIX) had soared +39.56%, while major U.S. stock averages (SP500)(COMP:IND)(DJI) plummeted across the board.     

U.S. President Donald Trump's sweeping reciprocal tariffs announcement has hammered sentiment across the globe. U.S. growth expectations have soured, and recession odds have jumped. The VIX's movement implies that market participants are only expecting it to get worse from here.   

"(Thursday’s) historic selling pressure in stock markets is not an overreaction, considering that recessions have generated significant drawdowns in equities in the past. An economic downturn is now an even chance, with odds rising the longer the administration maintains these trade measures. Meanwhile, the White House has accepted the risks of a painful transition. It is increasingly focused on medium and long-term horizons to achieve the difficult goals of manufacturing onshoring and fiscal balance," José Torres, senior economist at Interactive Brokers, said.  

"However, the short-term is looking especially gloomy, as cost pressures stemming from tariffs and austerity measures serving to reduce public sector employment and social programs are powerful forces that pose strong headwinds to consumer spending, hiring, and business investment," Torres added. 

The VIX (VIX) was last +22.39% at 36.74 points in Friday morning trade. 

Here are some exchange-traded funds focused on volatility: (BATS:VXX), (BATS:VIXY), (VXZ), (BATS:VIXM), (BATS:UVXY), (BATS:SVXY), and (BATS:UVIX).

And here are some ETFs tied to the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500): (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: AvalonBay Communities downgraded at BMO on higher costs, weaker demand
data.description:
Multi-family residential REIT AvalonBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) was downgraded at BMO Capital Markets on potentially higher expenses and weaker demand in its home market.

Shares were 4.11% down in Friday morning trade at $197.40.

The Arlington, VA-based real estate investment trust was cut to Market Perform from Outperform, with the price target reduced to $220.00 from $230.00.

"DOGE-related job cuts in Washington, DC, spiked in March to 217K, bringing YTD job cuts to 288K, according to the March Challenger report, which we estimate at 8.4% of total employment," said BMO analysts.

"While federal employees will receive severance that could mask dents in apartment fundamentals, we expect conditions to weaken during peak leasing season. AVB derives 15.6% of its SSNOI from DC Metro / Baltimore region," said the analysts.

 AVB's ambitious $3.5B development pipeline by the end of 2025 faces rising headwinds, according to the research note.

The anticipated rise in lumber tariffs is expected to drive up construction costs, while deportation may drive labor costs higher, according to the note.

Also, deportation is expected to impact class B and C rental demand, which is estimated to reduce the overall renter net absorption in the REIT's markets, the note said.

The stock rose ~17% in 2024, but is trading ~10% lower year-to-date.

BMO attributed the decline to rising development and DC risks, as well as recent capital allocation decisions.

AvalonBay gets a Buy rating from the Wall Street community as well as Seeking Alpha authors. Meanwhile, Quant grades the stock as Hold.

data.title: Renovaro says Predictive Oncology terminates merger transaction
data.description:
• Renovaro BioSciences (NASDAQ:RENB) has announced that it received an email from Predictive Oncology (NASDAQ:POAI) terminating the merger transaction.
  • The company added, Renovaro's position is that POI must comply with the binding obligations thereunder and enter into an exclusive License Agreement as required in each of the Binding Agreements.
  • Renovaro notes that POI is in breach of the Binding Agreements and has caused substantial damage to Renovaro for which it will seek redress.
  • Failure to enter into an exclusive License Agreement on the terms set forth in the Binding Agreement on or before April 10, 2025, will cause Renovaro to seek all its legal remedies to recover all its damages and/or seek additional remedies to fully redress the breaches.
  • POAI is -10.2% to $1.23.
  • RENB is -10.1% to $0.42.

data.title: Citi warns that Kraft Heinz may disappoint with its earnings report
data.description:
Citi downgraded Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) to a Sell rating as the firm took a cautious view of the upcoming Q1 earnings report. Analyst Thomas Palmer pointed to risks that the food company's organic sales growth will fall below the consensus estimate for the quarter.

"KHC’s measured takeaway growth continues to struggle, driven by share losses. And other channels and geographies, could face sales pressure, as well," warned Palmer.

Palmer and his team also expressed concern about worsening food service trends and warned that Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) might eventually need to reset its margin structure lower to drive volume. 

Citi lowered its price target on Kraft Heinz (KHC) to $27 from $28 to account for the lower earnings outlook 

Shares of Kraft Heinz (KHC) are up slightly for the week as the packaged food sector has attracted attention from investors as a defensive play. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 5.2%.

data.title: U.S. crude oil plunges 8% as China retaliates with tariffs, heating up global trade war
data.description:
Crude oil futures plunge for a second day on Friday, sinking to the lowest in four years during the depths of the COVID-19 pandemic, after China announced a retaliatory tariff of 34% on U.S. imports in an escalating trade war a day after President Trump unveiled tariffs that could weaken global demand while OPEC+ delivered a surprise production increase.

Imports of oil, gas and refined products were granted exemptions from Trump's new tariffs, but traders fear the policies could spark inflation, slow economic growth and intensify trade disputes, hurting demand for crude.

"China's aggressive countermove to U.S. tariffs all but confirms we are heading towards a global trade war; a war that has no winners and which will hurt economic growth and demand for key commodities such as crude oil and refined products," Saxo Bank head of commodity strategy Ole Hansen wrote.

The tariffs and OPEC+ production hike amount to a "very bad combo" for the crude market, Swissquote Bnak analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya said, and "as such, the supply/demand fundamentals remain comfortably negative."

Citing higher OPEC+ supply and the risk of an escalating trade conflict, Goldman Sachs lowered its 2025 average price forecasts for Brent and WTI crude to $69/bbl and $66/bbl, respectively, down from $73 and $69 previously.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for May delivery trades -8.5% at $61.27/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for June delivery -7.6% at $64.82/bbl.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (NYSEARCA:XLE)

Energy company shares are suffering heavy losses for a second straight day, including the top 10 energy names ranked by market value: Exxon Mobil (XOM) -4.4%, Chevron (CVX) -5.9%, ConocoPhillips (COP) -8.1%, Enterprise Products Partners (EPD) -6.2%, Williams Companies (WMB) -7.9%, EOG Resources (EOG) -6.3%, Kinder Morgan (KMI) -7.5%, Energy Transfer (ET) -9.5%, SLB (SLB) -9.3%, MPLX (MPLX) -7.3%.

data.title: Global tariff-sheltered stocks – stocks with no U.S. exposure
data.description:
BofA analysts gathered a group of global stocks that may be shielded from the negative impacts of U.S. tariff announcements.

These are large and liquid stocks, with a market cap of more than $10B, an average daily trading volume of more than $10M, 0% exposure to the U.S. as well as a negative sensitivity to the U.S. dollar, an “above-average” quality and “news” ranking, and the ability to outperform if the global earnings cycle slows, said Nigel Tupper, chief global quantitative strategist at BofA.

“Exporters to the U.S. have underperformed in most regions of the world,” he said. “U.S. tariffs appear one of the main differentiators of stock performance year-to-date.”

The following stocks are believed not to be negatively impacted by U.S. tariffs:

  • Coles Group (OTCPK:CLEGF)
  • Suncorp (OTCPK:SNMYF)
  • OMV AG (OTCPK:OMVJF)

data.title: Radius Recycling Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.99 beats by $0.02, revenue of $643M beats by $7.06M
data.description:
• Radius Recycling press release (NASDAQ:RDUS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.99 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $643M (+3.5% Y/Y) beats by $7.06M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was approximately break-even in the second quarter of fiscal 2025, compared to $3 million in the prior year second quarter.
